what_it_does: An IIFE that bridges legacy global picker arrays (WIDGET_KINDS, WIDGET_SYSTEMS, WIZARD_KINDS, STEP_KINDS, WS_BLOCKS) to the new CV_REGISTRY. It rebuilds each legacy array from registry queries (mapping Types into the legacy shapes), preserving original built-in entries, re-runs on every registry change and during boot, marks WS_BLOCKS with their type ids, and exposes CV_TYPES_SAVE (promote-instance helpers) and CV_TYPES_PROMPT (the save-as-Type modal channel).
observations: Direction is explicitly registry → legacy: the registry is source of truth, legacy arrays are mirrors.
Converter helpers (toWidgetKind/toWidgetSystem/toWizardKind/toStepKind) map a Type to each legacy consumer's shape, deriving id from runtime.key or the id tail.
rebuild() snapshots the original built-ins into window.__LEGACY_* the first time, then merges registry entries (override) with any legacy-only entries (kept, stamped provenance:'built-in').
toWizardKind infers shape from tags (hexagon/circle/octagon else diamond) and hydrates steps/shape from legacy when registry lacks them.
WS_BLOCKS renderers are not replaced — each block def is marked with _typeId 'block.<key>' so consumers can show the link.
Fires a 'cv-registry-rebuild' CustomEvent after each rebuild to notify watching UI.
Boot uses both a requestAnimationFrame-debounced schedule (on R.subscribe) and a setInterval polling up to 20 attempts to wait for Babel-loaded builders to populate legacy arrays.
CV_TYPES_SAVE.fromWidget/fromWizard/fromWizardStep/fromBlock all delegate to window.CV_TYPES_VI.promoteInstance with specific intoLayer/intoFamily.
CV_TYPES_PROMPT.open/subscribe is a tiny pub-sub feeding the SaveAsTypeModal.
standouts: The whole file exists to keep old code working unchanged while making the registry the single source — user/Vi-authored Types appear in legacy pickers automatically without touching the consumers.
It snapshots the original built-in arrays into __LEGACY_* so registry merges never lose hand-authored entries — an idempotent rebuild that tolerates being run repeatedly.
conventions: Upholds registry-as-truth: legacy arrays are derived mirrors, not parallel sources.
Warns (console.warn) and returns if CV_REGISTRY is missing rather than throwing — softer than the project's loud-fail rule.
Idempotent merge keyed by id with a 'seen' set, a recurring pattern across all four array rebuilds.
Defensive boot handling acknowledges the Babel-trickle load order documented in the project charter.
concerns: The per-rebuild try/catch around subscriber callbacks (in CV_TYPES_PROMPT.open) swallows errors silently (empty catch), which conflicts with the loud-fail rule.
The setInterval boot can call rebuild() multiple times before the rAF schedule path takes over; harmless given idempotency but it is two overlapping boot mechanisms.
Relies on window.CV_TYPES_VI existing when CV_TYPES_SAVE methods are called — no guard; will throw at call time if absent.
notes: This adapter keeps the legacy widget/wizard/step/block pickers populated from CV_REGISTRY so existing builders keep working while the registry is the one source of truth.
questions: What defines window.CV_TYPES_VI.promoteInstance and where is it loaded relative to this file?
Are the legacy arrays (WIDGET_KINDS etc.) consumed by code that re-reads them on the 'cv-registry-rebuild' event, or do some consumers cache the array reference?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — the header states it bridges legacy global constants to the registry with direction registry→legacy so user/Vi Types appear automatically, and the code implements exactly that plus the save/promote helpers.
apparent_themes: Interface as projection of the registry
intention_signals: Trying to migrate a mature app onto one registry without rewriting its consumers — make the new registry authoritative while the old picker arrays keep working as auto-maintained shadows.
novelty: The __LEGACY_* snapshot-then-merge strategy that preserves original built-ins across repeated registry-driven rebuilds, plus marking renderer blocks with their type id rather than replacing them.
contribution: Contributes the compatibility bridge that maps registry Types into legacy picker shapes and the promote-instance / save-as-Type plumbing (CV_TYPES_SAVE, CV_TYPES_PROMPT) that feed the modal.
summary_for_embedding: A bridge module that rebuilds legacy widget/wizard/step/block picker arrays from CV_REGISTRY queries and exposes promote-instance and save-as-Type helpers.
intention_for_embedding: To make the new Type registry the single source of truth while keeping legacy pickers and builders working unchanged by auto-mirroring registry Types into them.
